title: SecHound finding
description: One record per finding, de-duplicated by root cause. Domain-neutral: works for web, cloud, binary, secrets, dependency, mobile, or any security domain. Domain-specific fields (endpoint, etc.) are optional.

id.description: Stable ID, e.g. SH-<COMPONENT>-NNNN. Never reassigned.

title.description: Short description ending in the root cause.

status.description: candidate = unproven; confirmed = reproduced with evidence.

domain.description: Security domain, free-form. e.g. web, api, cloud, iac, binary, secrets, deps, mobile, llm. Drives which profile's controls apply.

category.description: Vulnerability class or rule id. Free-form (e.g. 'sql-injection', 'hardcoded-secret', 'use-after-free', 'public-s3-bucket') or a scanner ruleId.

cwe.description: Optional CWE id, e.g. 'CWE-89'. Universal taxonomy.

component.description: Logical grouping the finding belongs to (service, module, package, image, repo). Domain-defined. (Alias: 'service' is accepted for back-compat.)

service.description: Deprecated alias for 'component'. Still read by tooling.

location.description: Where the finding is, in whatever form fits the domain: 'path/File.ext:42', an HTTP route, a binary offset, a cloud resource ARN, a package@version.

invalidators.description: Controls that, if present, make this a false positive. Each must be checked, not assumed.

source.description: How it was found: manual | orchestrator | agent:<name> | sarif:<tool> | dast | <scanner>.

triage.description: Set by the LLM triage pass over imported/candidate findings.

repro.description: Re-runnable repro contract checked by verify_finding.
